person.name: Sir William de Englefield
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Sir William de Englefeld, who was living in the time of Richard I, was the son of Sir Alan de Englefeld.

William de Englefeud was the son of Alan, the son of Emma, the daughter of Alan de Dunsterville and his second wife, Muriel.

In 27 Henry III [28 October 1242-27 October 1243], William de Englefeud, Alan Basset and Gilbert de Boseville sued Robert Danvers and his wife, Muriel, for half a knight's fee, excepting two carucates of land, in Schiplak, and the advowson of the church. William's grandmother, Emma, was the sister of Alan's mother, Cecily; Gilbert's mother, Alice; Muriel, the defendant; and Geoffrey de Dunstanville. Geoffrey was seised of the properties in the time of Henry II and died without having surviving children. Muriel and Robert Danvers pleaded that the land was the inheritance of Muriel, the second wife of Alan de Dunsterville, and the mother of Muriel Danvers, Emma, Cecily and Alice. The plaintiffs denied that Muriel Danvers was a coheir of her brother Geoffrey de Dunstanville, receiving a verdict in their favour except for the land which was Muriel's marriage portion.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de Englefield於大約1174年出生在英國波克夏恩格利費爾德時，他的父親Sir Alan Englefield22歲，母親Maud De Englefield22歲。他於大約1194年與Lady Emma de Dunstanville, Heiress of Shiplake在英國波克夏恩格利費爾德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1260年在英國享年87歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Englefield
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from either of two places called Englefield (in Berks and Surrey).  Compare  Inglefield  .

variants.name: Inglefield
meanings: English: locative name from Englefield (Surrey), recorded as Ingelfeld in 1282, or Englefield (Berks).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de Englefield was born about 1174, in Englefield, Berkshire, England, his father, Sir Alan Englefield, was 22 and his mother, Maud De Englefield, was 22. He married Lady Emma de Dunstanville, Heiress of Shiplake about 1194, in Englefield, Berkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died in 1260, in England, at the age of 87.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William d’Englefield est né environ 1174, à Englefield, Berkshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Alan Englefield, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Maud De Englefield, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Lady Emma de Dunstanville, Heiress of Shiplake environ 1194, à Englefield, Berkshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1260, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 87 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de Englefield(이)가 대략 1174년 영국 버크실르 잉글레필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Alan Englefield(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud De Englefield(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1194년 영국 버크실르 잉글레필드에서 Lady Emma de Dunstanville, Heiress of Shiplake(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1260년 영국에서 87세의 나이로 사망했다.
